evals.prompt: ok so life is chaos right now. the kids school sends like 30 emails a week, soccer schedule changed twice, my wife and i keep double booking weekends and i missed a dentist appointment for my son last month. i dont even know what i need honestly. can you just take this over and sort it out
evals.expected_output: AI-Ready Brief with inferred intent/goal + at least one user-outcome success criterion + labeled assumptions + exactly ONE clarifying question (scope/access fork) with options and a recommended default. No execution before confirmation.

evals.prompt: i need a presentation about my startup for next week. make it good, maybe 10 slides or so. it needs to really land
evals.expected_output: Brief with verifiable success criteria including one outcome check; exactly ONE question targeting audience/purpose (the real fork), NOT format/length which the user already gave. Assumptions labeled with confidence.

evals.prompt: in ./testdata/notes.txt make an uppercase copy saved as notes_upper.txt in the same folder
evals.expected_output: NO brief, NO clarifying questions - trivially clear single-step task. Just do it and confirm. Tests that SUITS passes through instead of adding ceremony.

evals.prompt: Help me manage my projects so i know what to do each week
evals.expected_output: Brief capturing a recurring weekly prioritization system as the real goal; ONE question about where projects/tasks currently live (source-of-truth fork); labeled assumptions.

evals.prompt: We just bought this laundry and want to let the people in the community know about the laundry
evals.expected_output: Brief with intent = win first local customers (not 'send information'); at least one user-outcome success criterion; ONE question with options + recommended default; no invented business facts.

evals.prompt: we're launching the paid tier of my product and honestly i have no idea how to price it. don't just hand me options - interview me, ask me whatever you need until WE figure out what i actually want
evals.expected_output: Level 2 interview mode: exactly ONE question in round 1 targeting the most upstream fork, with options and a recommended answer; visible open-fork ledger; exit ramp offered ('enough - proceed' converts open forks to labeled assumptions).
